content:
# Daily Waste Log

**Tool ID:** T097-01
**Block:** B097 (Stock Management Excellence)
**Domain:** Operations & Workflow (D05_operations_workflow)
**Level:** L1
**Format:** Tracker
**Role:** All Staff
**Time Required:** 1–2 minutes per entry
**Version:** 1.0

---
## Welcome

Every item that goes in the bin is money leaving the business. A croissant thrown away at closing is not just the cost of the croissant — it is the cost of the ingredients, the labour to make or order it, and the shelf space it occupied all day. This tracker makes waste visible so you can reduce it.

Log it when it happens. One line, thirty seconds. The patterns that emerge will save you hundreds per month.

**Built for All Staff working in Operations & Workflow.**
Use it. Adapt it. Make it yours.

---

## Purpose

A simple, shift-by-shift waste log that captures every discarded item with its reason, quantity, and estimated cost. Feeds into weekly and monthly waste analysis to identify patterns and drive reduction.

This tool supports **Stock Management Excellence** (B097) and is designed to be practical, printable, and immediately useful in a working cafe environment.

---

## How to Use This Tool

**Who:** All Staff (every person who discards an item logs it)
**Time Required:** 1–2 minutes per entry; 5 minutes for shift-end summary
**Frequency:** Continuously throughout each shift. Summary at shift end.

**What You'll Need:**
- This log (printed on a clipboard near the waste bin, or on a tablet)
- Pen
- Awareness of approximate item costs (reference table below)

**Steps:**
1. Every time you discard an item, log it immediately — do not wait until end of shift
2. Record the item, quantity, reason, and estimated cost
3. At shift end, total the waste value and sign off
4. Shift lead reviews the log and flags any unusual patterns to the manager
5. Manager reviews daily totals weekly and acts on trends

---

## Tracker

### Date: ________ | Shift: AM / PM / Full Day

### Waste Log Entries

| Time | Item Discarded | Qty | Unit | Reason Code | Est. Cost (EUR) | Logged By |
|------|---------------|-----|------|-------------|-----------------|-----------|
| *07:15* | *Espresso shot (over-extracted)* | *1* | *shot* | *PREP* | *0.30* | *MK* |
| *08:40* | *Oat milk (expired yesterday)* | *2* | *litres* | *EXP* | *3.80* | *JP* |
| *09:15* | *Croissant (dropped on floor)* | *1* | *pc* | *DAM* | *0.90* | *AL* |
| *10:30* | *Flat white (wrong order — made with dairy instead of oat)* | *1* | *drink* | *ERR* | *1.20* | *MK* |
| *14:00* | *Avocado (overripe, brown inside)* | *3* | *pcs* | *EXP* | *2.40* | *RS* |
| *17:30* | *Muffins (unsold end of day)* | *4* | *pcs* | *EOD* | *4.00* | *JP* |
| | | | | | | |
| | | | | | | |
| | | | | | | |
| | | | | | | |
| | | | | | | |
| | | | | | | |

### Reason Codes

| Code | Meaning | Examples |
|------|---------|---------|
| **PREP** | Preparation waste | Over-extracted shots, burnt milk, failed latte art practice, over-portioned |
| **EXP** | Expired / out of date | Past use-by date, milk turned, produce spoiled |
| **DAM** | Damaged / contaminated | Dropped, spilt, cross-contaminated, packaging torn |
| **ERR** | Order error / remake | Wrong drink made, wrong food item, customer complaint remake |
| **EOD** | End-of-day discard | Unsold pastries, display items past safe holding time, leftover prepped food |
| **RET** | Customer return | Sent back by customer (taste, temperature, allergy concern) |
| **OVR** | Over-production | Too much batch brew made, too many sandwiches prepped |

### Quick Cost Reference

| Item | Approx. Cost (EUR) |
|------|-------------------|
| Espresso shot (18g beans) | 0.25–0.35 |
| Milk (per litre, dairy) | 1.00–1.30 |
| Oat milk (per litre) | 1.80–2.20 |
| Croissant | 0.70–1.00 |
| Pain au chocolat | 0.80–1.10 |
| Muffin | 0.60–0.90 |
| Slice of cake | 1.00–1.80 |
| Avocado | 0.60–0.90 |
| Sourdough slice | 0.30–0.50 |
| Takeaway cup + lid | 0.08–0.15 |
| Sandwich (assembled) | 1.50–2.50 |

### Shift Summary

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Total items wasted | *e.g., 12* |
| Total estimated waste cost (EUR) | *e.g., 12.60* |
| Top waste category (reason code) | *e.g., EOD (end-of-day)* |
| Top wasted item | *e.g., muffins (4 pcs)* |
| Shift lead initials | |
| Manager reviewed? | Yes / No |

### Waste Targets

| Metric | Daily Target | Acceptable Range | Action Trigger |
|--------|-------------|-----------------|----------------|
| Total waste cost | < EUR 15 | EUR 10–20 | > EUR 20 = manager review same day |
| Waste as % of revenue | < 2% | 1–3% | > 3% = investigate top 3 items |
| Prep waste (PREP + ERR) | < EUR 5 | EUR 2–7 | > EUR 7 = retraining needed |
| End-of-day waste (EOD) | < EUR 8 | EUR 5–10 | > EUR 10 = reduce next-day order |

---

## Common Risks

### Risk 1: Staff Don't Log Waste in Real Time

**Why it happens:** Logging feels like extra work during a busy shift. Items get binned without recording.
**How to prevent:** Place the clipboard directly next to the waste bin. Make it physically impossible to throw something away without seeing the log. Reinforce during team briefings.
**If it's already happening:** Start with end-of-day waste only (easiest to count). Add real-time logging once the habit forms.

### Risk 2: Cost Estimates Are Wildly Inaccurate

**Why it happens:** Staff guess costs rather than using the reference table. A "small" waste feels like nothing but adds up.
**How to prevent:** Print the Quick Cost Reference on the back of the log sheet. Even rough accuracy is better than no tracking.
**If it's already happening:** Have the manager pre-fill cost estimates for your top 20 items.

### Risk 3: Waste Data Collected But Never Analysed

**Why it happens:** Logs stack up in a drawer. Nobody reviews them.
**How to prevent:** Schedule a 10-minute weekly waste review (see T097-02). Look for the top 3 wasted items and ask "why" for each.
**If it's already happening:** Pull last week's logs right now. Total them. Share the number with the team. That single act often triggers change.

---

## Customisation Notes

**This tool is a starting point, not a rulebook.**

**Independent / Specialty Cafe:** Add rows for specialty items (cold brew concentrate, house syrups). Track barista practice waste separately if training new staff.
**Chain / Franchise:** Align reason codes with corporate waste categories. Some chains require photo evidence — add a column for photo reference if needed.
**Kiosk / Mobile:** Simpler menu means fewer items to track. Focus on coffee waste (shots, milk) and end-of-day food.
**Bakery Cafe:** End-of-day waste will be your largest category. Track by individual bake item to optimise production quantities.

**Making it yours:**
- Adjust the Quick Cost Reference to match your actual supplier prices
- Add a "Could this have been saved?" column for waste reduction ideas
- Colour-code reason codes on the printed version for quick scanning
- Add a weekly total row at the bottom for easy aggregation

The structure matters more than the specific fields. Keep the structure, change the content.

---

## Connected Resources

**Other tools in this block:**
- T097-02 — Stock Management Dashboard (L2, dashboard)
- T097-03 — Demand Forecasting Worksheet (L3, template)
- T097-04 — Monthly Stock Management Scorecard (L3, dashboard)

**Related blocks:**

- B096

**Parent block:** B097 — Stock Management Excellence
**Module:** M05: Service Flow & Peak Management
---

*Tool Version 1.0 — OIS Cafe v2*
*Built for cafe operators who value clarity, consistency, and craft.*
